@schinskytrades @mwfowlie Except that's what they said it was for! For example, see the paragraph beginning "A market on" in this post:

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman I'm not saying that wouldn't be a good rule to have. I am saying that the claim that it's obvious that it's the case seems quite absurd without some citation to some authority holding so.

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman It is not obvious to me that given that Congress has the power to create or destroy courts inferior to the Supreme Court, they nevertheless don't have the power to monitor with little or no restriction what goes on in those inferior courts.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi Also, to address the libel issue more directly, we don't hold people liable for libel absent some evidence of wrongdoing. If, with no wrongdoing, I libel you and cause you severe damages, you still have no remedy at law in the United States.

Why do we do this? Because we don't
@mrddmia @RonColeman Do you have any citation to precedential holdings that make it obvious that they couldn't do so even if a statute says they can? It's certainly not obvious to me.
@reeeesistance These guys get a pass though. https://t.co/F5aWGtFWG7
@Britannica "Holmes?! Are you in bed with some sort of a schoolgirl?!"

@7_paradigm I think it's because this comes from something that's been in the works since a bit before the acquisition was 100% final due to some straggling regulatory approvals.
@JesterJum Forrrest Gump
@BallerToy1327 The first episode is so unbelievably good.
Seems important. https://t.co/tSiTsdlS3S

@Vet_X0 @AbsGEC @IOHK_Charles @bgarlinghouse @digitalassetbuy @DigPerspectives @johnnykrypto00 Ripple had a lot of opportunities where we could have advocated only for ourselves to the detriment of those who would follow us. We absolutely try as hard as we possibly can not to do that, I don't think we ever have done that, but I also think we would do that if we had to. We
@analyst_404 @AriDavidPaul @RecTheRegime I love how you have to put "in this instance" at the end otherwise reality completely destroys your point.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi And it's quite possible that speaking by embedding your views in an AI could become (some might argue, already is) one of the most effective ways to spread ideas and advocate for them. It is likely no less important as a form of speech as television and the internet.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 14% due to: active Hormuz disruption event (Rumaila shutdown, shipping suspended), compound stress approaching CRITICAL, XRP at $1.34 (approaching $1 bear threshold), Extreme Fear at 14, and ECB/Dimon regulatory headwinds for stablecoins. Mid case increased from 38% to 32% — wait, correcting: mid should increase slightly because the Hormuz crisis simultaneously validates the institutional settlement infrastructure thesis (the paradox). Revising: Base reduced from 40% to 42% reflects short-term damage offset by infrastructure demand thesis. Mid reduced from 38% to 32% as the extreme macro uncertainty compresses higher-probability outcomes. Bull held at 12% — Hormuz could be the catalyst but too early to increase. Net: more weight in tails (bear up, mid down), reflecting increased volatility and binary outcome potential.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Elevated from prior assessment. XRP at $1.34 is approaching the <$1 bear case threshold. Active geopolitical crisis creating severe macro headwinds. Competing infrastructure (BOJ blockchain, Ondo/ADGM, Kinexys) advancing without XRPL. Stablecoin regulatory headwinds building from both ECB and JPMorgan. ODL data remains completely opaque. PermissionedDEX has zero adoption 11 days post-launch.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new competitive signals but SWIFT's existing 11,500-bank network remains the default. Every day without ODL volume data is a day SWIFT's incumbency advantage compounds.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new developments detected. Visa's B2B settlement product remains operational but has not captured significant cross-border market share against SWIFT or blockchain alternatives.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Dimon's push for bank-like stablecoin regulation would give JPMorgan's regulated infrastructure a structural advantage over non-bank competitors. Kinexys benefits from any regulatory tightening on crypto-native settlement.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BOJ blockchain settlement experiment could integrate with BIS Project Nexus framework — sovereign-to-sovereign settlement without private bridge tokens. If BOJ builds independent blockchain settlement, this reduces one of XRPL's key demand drivers.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo digital securities achieving ADGM regulatory approval and Binance MTF listing demonstrates institutional RWA tokenization advancing on non-XRPL infrastructure. This directly competes with XRPL PermissionedDEX's value proposition. 11 days post-launch with zero XRPL adoption vs. Ondo's regulated listing is a concerning signal.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume remains completely opaque — no public data available. This kill switch has been 'NEEDS_DATA' for months. The absence of transparency itself is a bearish signal: if volumes were impressive, there would be incentive to publish them.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: Token velocity data remains unknown. XRP's $3.1B 24h volume against $82B market cap gives a velocity ratio of 3.8% — but this is exchange trading volume, not utility settlement. Without ODL data, there is no way to distinguish speculative from utility-driven velocity.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Hormuz disruption + oil supply shock + trade war tariffs + extreme fear creates stagflationary environment. Energy cost spike will compress global economic activity. Recession risk is no longer speculative.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: Fear & Greed at 14 (Extreme Fear). XRP down from ~$3+ peaks to $1.34. Broad risk-off environment with active geopolitical crisis. If XRP breaks below $1, bear case probability spikes sharply.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: ECB stablecoin warning + Dimon bank-regulation push signals potential regulatory tightening cycle for stablecoins. EU could impose restrictive framework that constrains RLUSD growth in European markets.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: Oil supply shock is inflationary — could delay or reverse expected rate cuts. US 10Y at 3.97% suggests some flight to safety, but an inflationary oil shock could push the Fed hawkish, extending restrictive conditions.

bear_case.bear_narrative: COMPOUND STRESS UPGRADED TO CRITICAL. Hormuz disruption events (Iraq Rumaila shutdown, CMA CGM Middle East suspension) trigger the established CRITICAL threshold by rule — operator action window measured in days, not weeks. Bear pressure at 62: the thesis faces a three-way squeeze from an information void (ODL data null, ETF AUM null, PermissionedDEX too early), Ripple's own strategic de-emphasis of XRP (confirmed across four independent signals), and an actively cascading geopolitical stress chain with stale data masking likely severity. The paradox is live and unresolvable: Hormuz crisis simultaneously destroys XRP price as a risk asset AND validates the need for the settlement infrastructure XRP is designed to provide. HOLD with INCREASED MONITORING — no kill switch has triggered, architectural thesis pillars remain intact, and extreme fear is the worst moment for tactical exits — but the next 72 hours of Brent price data and Hormuz developments will determine whether this assessment holds or escalates to REDUCE_EXPOSURE.
